calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Farinas appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

The Court questions why the ex parte petitions for withdrawal of funds from the blocked accounts are being filed in the Civil Court and noted that the amount of time spent on the ex parte petitions which range from 5.0 hours to 11.5 hours is excessive. Ms. Farinas stated that it was written in the civil order that the Civil Court would be reviewing withdrawals from the blocked accounts and that they are working with the Petitioner to teach him how to do it himself to lower fees. The Court noted that back in 2017 Counsel had discussion with this Court to file these petitions in Probate, that Counsel raised the issue of the bank wanting certain things and that he was told to find out what the bank wanted. Ms. Farinas stated that they are not sure how to get the ex parte petitions transferred to Probate and that she is not sure what has been done. Ms. Farinas also addressed the $2,800.00 charge for the accountings stating that this petition included three accountings and that if you split the amount it comes out to $930.00 per accounting. The Court stated that this could have been avoided by requesting waivers of accountings at a previous accounting, that there was a lot of time spent communicating with the Civil Department as to the status of the orders, that these types of things could have been avoided by filing in the Probate Department and that 30 minutes for checking online documents is excessive. Ms. Farinas stated that perhaps the time was not described properly and were for more than just looking at the docket, that the trusts range from $21,000.00 to $28,000.00, that not coming in for accountings will cut costs and that they are confused as to where to request fees. The Court clarified that fees are to be requested in Probate. The Second Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney to appear on May 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear and why the second accounting has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
